diagrams.title: Virtual Address Translation in Paging
diagrams.description: Shows how a virtual address is translated to physical address using page table
diagrams.purpose: Understand paging address translation mechanism and role of page tables

diagrams.title: Page Table Entry Structure
diagrams.description: Illustrates the structure of a single page table entry with all control bits
diagrams.purpose: Learn what information is stored in page tables and how protection/management is achieved

diagrams.title: Internal vs External Fragmentation
diagrams.description: Comparison diagram showing internal and external fragmentation in memory
elements: Internal: fixed block with unused space inside
External: allocated blocks with scattered free gaps
Compaction: moving blocks to create one large free area
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the two types of memory fragmentation and their differences

diagrams.title: TLB and Page Table Interaction
diagrams.description: Shows the flow of address translation with TLB cache and page table fallback
elements: Page table lookup in memory (slower) if TLB miss
diagrams.purpose: Understand how TLB improves performance and when page table access is needed
